title: eryngium dichotomum
intro: Eryngium dichotomum, also known as forked sea holly, is a plant species in the family Apiaceae, native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Eryngium dichotomum include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote digestive health. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine to support overall well-being.
